## Analysis

CS Major intention breakdown:
  No: 713
  Yes - BS: 21
  Yes - Minor: 18
  Yes - BA: 12

## Conclusion

We found that non cs major students' interest in CS in other fields was greater than or equal to  CS majors across levels 4-7. While that is expected, because CS major students would be more likely to be interested in CS alone, the high level of engagement provides an initial promise for demand for our videos. There are also moderately significant proportions of students with little to no interest in connecting to other fields, but that is to be expected. We have no expectation of every student watching the videos, as well as if they are of high quality, word of mouth could spread interest. 

# Analysis:
CS Major intention breakdown:
  No: 713
  Yes - BS: 21
  Yes - Minor: 18
  Yes - BA: 12
  
<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="<custom-path>/static/imgs/logo.png" alt="Image of Comp110 rainbow logo. "  width="500"/>

## Conclusion:

This is basic paragrapWe found that non cs major students' interest in CS in other fields was greater than or equal to  CS majors across levels 4-7. While that is expected, because CS major students would be more likely to be interested in CS alone, the high level of engagement provides an initial promise for demand for our videos. There are also moderately significant proportions of students with little to no interest in connecting to other fields, but that is to be expected. We have no expectation of every student watching the videos, as well as if they are of high quality, word of mouth could spread interest. 


Next we look by major, and see the other majors with the high interest across the values: interested_connection, valuable, interesting, social_issue, and tech_best_interestes. Excluding a few majors such as physics and earth science, most majors see an average above 4 for all categories, which show possible signs of significant engagement. 

In our last graph, we see that for social issues and their relevance to CS category, responses are not normally distributed, but centered around the high 6-7 values. And a slightly more normally distributed graph for the best interests category, but still skewed to be centered around 4-5. This shows that people are interested in connecting code to the outside world, and utilizing this to improve the standard of life around them. 

There is no exact, would watch videos category, which makes it hard to conclusively determine anything. However, based upon the data we gathered, it seems to be the case that there is real interest in connecting computer science to individuals careers, as well as the world around them in general. We would absolutely recommend providing videos with examples of how code is used across majors, ideally with some anecdotal evidence, and broad concept demonstration. One drawback here is that we do not expect the TA’s and Professors to have extensive knowledge of all majors, which would require recruiting from across departments. We have no real concept about how cooperative all the other professors are, but we imagine you would be able to find somebody in most departments who would be willing to vouch for the utility of computer science. The videos would also be useful for years to come, as the industries only change so much year to year, requiring a frontload of work, but from then on, they might just need minor tweaks or updates as technology evolves.
